cards.name: Do expenses #important
Redesign refund request and treasury handoff process
cards.description:
Status: In Progress

Progress:
☑ Added migration sql/033_refunds_add_status_tracking.sql with status tracking columns
☑ Created update-refund-status.json workflow to update status via webhook
☑ Created refund-status-form.json workflow with full status management UI
☑ Updated refund-form.json with link to status management form
☑ Update add-refund workflow to set initial status to 'Requested'
☑ Add member calculation email template and workflow
☑ Add 'Send Calculation Email to Member' button in status form
☑ Wrote docs/EXECPLAN-refund-redesign.md to reset the redesign around the unreleased-feature assumption
☑ Replaced the refund lifecycle with New Request / Request Bank Details / Bank Details Obtained / Submitted for Refund / Refund Processed / Refund Rejected
☑ Switched the live local landing page to /webhook/refunds
☑ Reworked refund-form into the new create flow with landing-page return URL handling
☑ Reworked the refund preview/send path into the shared template-backed HTML flow
☑ Repaired the local n8n publish/version state so the redesigned refund workflows are actually served in production mode
☑ Added delete-refund support from the refunds landing page
☑ Turned refund email tracking ids into clickable links from the landing page
☑ Added searchable membership-category suggestions to the refund create form while preserving free text entry
☐ Test full lifecycle end-to-end

Scope:
• Redesign the refund workflow so member communication happens before treasury handoff
• Calculate the refund when the request is raised
• Notify the originating member of the proposed refund and request bank details
• Only send the treasury request once bank details have been received
• Preserve current test-mode behaviour for outbound email

Recommended process:
• Capture a refund request as a refund case.
• Calculate the proposed refund and store the calculation and explanation.
• Email the originating member with the proposed refund amount and request their bank details.
• Record that bank details have been received against the refund case.
• Prepare and preview the treasury request from the refund case.
• Send the treasury request.
• Mark the case as paid once payment is confirmed.

Recommended case model:
• requested_by
• refund_for
• reason
• membership
• amount_paid
• from_date
• to_date
• months
• refund_amount
• calculation_explanation
• request_email_message_id
• bank_details_message_id
• treasury_email_message_id
• status
• notes
• created_at
• updated_at

Recommended statuses:
• Requested
• Calculated
• Awaiting Bank Details
• Bank Details Received
• Ready For Treasury
• Sent To Treasury
• Paid
• Rejected
• Cancelled

Design notes:
• Use a case-based process rather than treating the treasury email as the first step.
• Keep member communication and treasury communication as separate flows joined by a refund case record.
• Prefer not to store full bank details in the main refunds table.
• Safer audit fields are bank-details received timestamp, source message id, and sender address.
• If bank details must be visible to operators, prefer storing only a redacted summary in the database.
• This is still an unreleased feature, so we do not need rollout compatibility or old saved-link preservation.

Planned work:
• Review the current refunds table, templates, and workflows against the case-based model.
• Redesign the refunds schema around statuses, message ids, and audit fields.
• Add a member-facing email template for refund calculation and bank-details request.
• Add a workflow to create or update refund cases and calculate refunds.
• Add a workflow to send the member calculation email.
• Add an operator step to mark bank details as received without storing full bank details.
• Update the treasury preview and send flow to use refund cases instead of raw form input.
• Add a completion step for Paid and any reporting fields needed.
• Test the full lifecycle in test mode end to end.
• Backfill existing refund rows into the revised status model if needed.

Progress update:
• Corrected live refund-form webhook drift back to the stale inactive workflow.
• Updated refunds page email tracking links to use absolute Gmail mailbox URLs built from the configured refund mailbox, instead of generic account-index links.
• Made Delete Refund a collapsible disclosure on the refunds page to reduce accidental deletion risk.

Not in scope for this planning note:
• Implementing schema changes
• Modifying n8n workflows
• Changing live email templates
• Backfilling or migrating current refund rows

Platform comparison: Zammad vs ERPNext

Recommendation:
• ERPNext is the better fit if the club wants a structured, auditable refund-case workflow.
• Zammad is the better fit if the club wants the process to stay mostly email-driven and lightweight.

Why:
• The refund process includes calculation, case lifecycle, waiting for bank details, internal treasury handoff, and final payment tracking.
• That is closer to case management or workflow management than a classic support inbox.

Zammad:
• Strong for inbound and outbound email handling.
• Strong for agents working from a ticket queue.
• Good audit trail on the conversation itself.
• Weaker for calculation-heavy structured workflows.
• Treasury handoff would be another ticket event rather than a first-class business step.
• Custom fields and states can support the process, but the model would feel bolted on.

Best Zammad version of the process:
• ticket type Refund Request
• custom fields for membership, amount, months, refund, and bank-details-received
• triggers or macros for member email and treasury email
• operators manually move the ticket through states

ERPNext:
• Strong for a custom Refund Request record with fields, statuses, and workflow transitions.
• Strong for templated emails driven from structured case data.
• Strong for dashboards and reporting by reason, season, status, and paid date.
• Heavier to run and configure than Zammad.
• More up-front setup, but a better fit for a durable internal process.

Best ERPNext version of the process:
• custom Refund Request document
• fields for requester, member, reason, dates, amount paid, calculated refund, and status
• workflow states such as Requested, Awaiting Bank Details, Bank Details Received, Sent To Treasury, and Paid
• actions or buttons to send the member calculation email and the treasury request email

Pragmatic recommendation for Avondale:
• If the club wants a proper operations platform and expects this pattern to expand into other admin workflows, ERPNext is the better choice.
• If the club wants the quickest email-centric solution, Zammad is the better choice.
• If refunds are the only workflow under consideration, the current n8n + Postgres approach is still likely the most pragmatic path.

Data-handling note:
• Full bank details should preferably not be stored in either system unless the club explicitly wants that security and compliance burden.
• A safer pattern is to store that bank details were requested, that they were received, the source message id, and a redacted summary only if needed.

cards.name: Add Metabase report PDF form and export workflow
cards.description:
Summary

Implemented the repository artifacts for an operator-facing report flow that:

• serves an HTML form from n8n
• lists Metabase dashboard tab names to include in the report
• reuses the existing clubspark-exporter Playwright service to render dashboard tabs
• passes the merged PDF through Stirling PDF for sanitize and optional auto-redact

Repository changes

• added scripts/export-metabase-dashboard-pdf.mjs
• extended scripts/clubspark-export-server.mjs with POST /metabase-dashboard-pdf
• added workflows/metabase-report-form.json
• added workflows/metabase-report-generate.json
• seeded global_settings entries in sql/037_metabase_report_pdf_settings.sql
• updated exporter, workflow, and services documentation

Follow-up

• deploy the updated clubspark-exporter image on the n8n host
• apply sql/037_metabase_report_pdf_settings.sql
• import the two new workflow JSON files into n8n
• set METABASE_EMAIL and METABASE_PASSWORD for the exporter service
• validate the tab-click selectors against the live Metabase dashboard after deployment

cards.name: Choose typed query contract mechanism for Node scripts
Automate team captain contact list mailout
cards.description:
Goal

Generate a repeatable send manifest from the Teams script, sync the PDFs and manifest to the n8n server, and send captain emails through a dedicated workflow.

Requirements

• Test mode first only.
• One rerun script for generation, sync, and trigger.
• Attach own team, next team down, and reserves PDFs.
• Include intended recipient note in test mode output email.

Outcome

• Generator writes captain mailout manifest files on every run.
• Sync script copies the bundle to the n8n host and into the container file area used by file nodes.
• Runner script regenerates, syncs, and triggers the mailout in one step.
• Local n8n workflow Send Team Captain Contact Lists is active.
• Verified in test mode only: 17 emails sent to the test recipient with intended-recipient comments.

Improve batch email actions and templates
Expose batch completion from batch actions page
Add confirmation step for missing signup backfill
cards.description:
Goal

• Add a confirmation HTML page before creating a missing signup backfill.
• Require explicit category selection when more than one current valid membership exists.

Outcome

• Added Confirm Missing Signup Capture as a new webhook HTML page.
• Updated Member Search Detail so missing signup creation routes through the confirmation page.
• Hardened Create Missing Signup Capture so it only backfills current, active, paid latest-season memberships and excludes Pavilion Key.

Verification

• member-search-detail?member=John Robbie&membership=1. Senior 2026 shows 1. Senior 2026 as the primary membership and Captured.
• confirm-missing-signup-capture?member=John Robbie&membership=1. Senior 2026 renders HTML and correctly disables the already-captured option.
• create-missing-signup-capture?member=John Robbie&product=1. Senior 2025 now returns 404 not_found.

cards.name: Split missing-signup backfill into create-only and create-plus-batch actions
cards.description:
Completed on 2026-03-30.

Outcome

• The confirmation page for missing signup capture now offers two explicit actions.
• Create Signup Only creates or refreshes the backfilled signup capture without adding it to the current processing batch.
• Create Signup + Add to Current Batch preserves the old batching behavior when it is explicitly wanted.
• The create workflow now respects the add_to_batch choice instead of batching every inserted or refreshed backfill automatically.

Why

This prevents members who were already handled manually in an earlier completed batch from being silently re-added to the current batch when their signup capture is backfilled later.
